summary: 英国首相基尔·斯塔默宣布辞职，因工党在地方选举中惨败，党内压力迫使下台，曼彻斯特市长安迪·伯纳姆被视为继任热门。特朗普声称林肯纪念堂倒影池遭人为破坏，多人被捕，但未提供证据，批评者认为问题源于修复工程失败。世界杯亮点：库拉索等小国爆冷，文化融合成焦点。

vocabulary.en: an act of giving up a job or position
vocabulary.excerpt: Keir Starmer says he is resigning.
vocabulary.example_cn: 基尔·斯塔默说他将辞职。

vocabulary.en: increasing or growing pressure
vocabulary.excerpt: The British prime minister announcing his resignation in the last hour after mounting pressure to step aside.
vocabulary.example_cn: 英国首相在面临日益增加的下台压力后，于最后一小时宣布辞职。

vocabulary.en: to resign from a position or allow someone else to take over
vocabulary.excerpt: mounting pressure to step aside.
vocabulary.example_cn: 日益增加的下台压力。

vocabulary.en: the decisive or conclusive factor
vocabulary.excerpt: the clinching factor for this, Audie, after months of difficulties
vocabulary.example_cn: Audie，经过数月的困难，决定性因素是……

vocabulary.en: elections for local government positions
vocabulary.excerpt: we had local elections across the U.K. in various parts in May
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们在五月份英国各地举行了地方选举。

vocabulary.en: to increase suddenly and strongly
vocabulary.excerpt: the right-wing populist Reform Party, led by Nigel Farage, surge.
vocabulary.example_cn: 由奈杰尔·法拉奇领导的右翼民粹主义改革党支持率激增。

vocabulary.en: an event that warns someone to take action
vocabulary.excerpt: That was a real wake-up call.
vocabulary.example_cn: 那是一个真正的警钟。

vocabulary.en: on the other hand; conversely
vocabulary.excerpt: But on the flip side, we also have this issue where Andy Burnham...
vocabulary.example_cn: 但另一方面，我们还有安迪·伯纳姆的问题……

vocabulary.en: an election held to fill a vacancy that occurs between regular elections
vocabulary.excerpt: There was a special election that was held in the Northwest of England last week
vocabulary.example_cn: 上周在英格兰西北部举行了一场特别选举。

vocabulary.en: to come together to form one group or mass
vocabulary.excerpt: it all coalesced around this point.
vocabulary.example_cn: 所有因素都围绕这一点汇聚。

vocabulary.en: a smooth and organized transfer of power or responsibility
vocabulary.excerpt: I will do everything I can to ensure an orderly handover of power.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我将尽一切努力确保权力的有序交接。

vocabulary.en: clear and unambiguous support
vocabulary.excerpt: I will also give my successor my full and unequivocal support
vocabulary.example_cn: 我还将给予我的继任者充分且明确的支持。

vocabulary.en: the third attempt is likely to succeed
vocabulary.excerpt: Burnham, this will be basically third time's the charm
vocabulary.example_cn: 伯纳姆，这基本上会是第三次好运。

vocabulary.en: to attack or criticize aggressively
vocabulary.excerpt: Farage has come out swinging today
vocabulary.example_cn: 法拉奇今天猛烈抨击。

vocabulary.en: the authority to act granted by voters
vocabulary.excerpt: Andy Burnham doesn't ... have a mandate to lead.
vocabulary.example_cn: 安迪·伯纳姆没有领导授权。

vocabulary.en: the deliberate destruction of property
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump blames vandals for his Reflecting Pool problems.
vocabulary.example_cn: 特朗普总统将倒影池的问题归咎于破坏者。

vocabulary.en: an official summons or ticket for a minor offense
vocabulary.excerpt: they issued citations to five others.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他们向另外五人发出了传票。

vocabulary.en: a project to restore something to good condition
vocabulary.excerpt: his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed
vocabulary.example_cn: 他耗资1400万美元的修复工程失败了。

vocabulary.en: full of (something unpleasant)
vocabulary.excerpt: the water riddled with algae
vocabulary.example_cn: 水中充满藻类。

vocabulary.en: simple plants that grow in water
vocabulary.excerpt: the water riddled with algae
vocabulary.example_cn: 水中充满藻类。

vocabulary.en: to pursue legal action to the maximum degree allowed
vocabulary.excerpt: These are cases that will be prosecuted to the full extent.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这些案件将依法严惩。

vocabulary.en: a visually striking performance or display
vocabulary.excerpt: the World Cup now a must-see spectacle both on and off the field.
vocabulary.example_cn: 世界杯现在成为场内场外必看的奇观。

vocabulary.en: contrary to what common sense would suggest
vocabulary.excerpt: Is that smart or a counterintuitive strategy?
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是明智之举还是反直觉的策略？

vocabulary.en: to behave confidently and make others recognize their importance
vocabulary.excerpt: it is a moment for reform to try to assert themselves.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这是改革党试图彰显自身实力的时刻。

sentences.en: The British prime minister announcing his resignation in the last hour after mounting pressure to step aside.
sentences.cn: 英国首相在面临越来越大的下台压力后，于过去一小时内宣布辞职。
sentences.structure: 主句：The British prime minister announcing his resignation in the last hour；时间状语：after mounting pressure to step aside。
sentences.analysis: 现在分词短语作谓语，表示进行时；'mounting pressure' 是新闻常用表达，意为'日益增长的压力'；'step aside' 是'辞职'的委婉说法。

sentences.en: The question my party is asking now is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election.
sentences.cn: 我的政党现在提出的问题是，我是否是带领我们进入下一次大选的最佳人选。
sentences.structure: 主句：The question...is...；定语从句：my party is asking now；表语从句：whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election。
sentences.analysis: 'best placed' 是固定搭配，意为'处于最佳位置'；'lead into' 表示'带领进入'。

sentences.en: Every decision I've taken has been about putting the country I love first.
sentences.cn: 我所做的每一个决定都是为了把我热爱的国家放在首位。
sentences.structure: 主句：Every decision...has been about...；定语从句：I've taken；宾语：putting the country I love first。
sentences.analysis: 'putting...first' 是固定短语，意为'把...放在首位'；'I love' 是省略关系代词的定语从句修饰 'the country'。

sentences.en: That is why I will resign as leader of the Labour Party.
sentences.cn: 这就是我将辞去工党领袖职务的原因。
sentences.structure: 主句：That is；表语从句：why I will resign as leader of the Labour Party。
sentences.analysis: 'That is why...' 是常见句型，表示'这就是...的原因'；'resign as' 意为'辞去...职务'。

sentences.en: The real clinching factor was that we had local elections across the U.K. in various parts in May, and the Labour Party suffered a massive defeat, losing more than 1,400 local council seats, even as we saw the right-wing populist Reform Party, led by Nigel Farage, surge.
sentences.cn: 真正的决定性因素是，我们在5月份英国各地举行了地方选举，工党遭受了巨大失败，失去了1400多个地方议会席位，与此同时我们看到由奈杰尔·法拉奇领导的右翼民粹主义改革党崛起。
sentences.structure: 主句：The real clinching factor was that...；表语从句：we had local elections...and the Labour Party suffered...；伴随状语：losing more than 1,400 local council seats；时间状语从句：even as we saw...surge；插入语：led by Nigel Farage。
sentences.analysis: 'clinching factor' 是新闻用语，意为'决定性因素'；'massive defeat' 是'惨败'；'surge' 作动词表示'激增、崛起'；'led by' 是过去分词短语作定语。

sentences.en: That seemed to suggest to certain parts of the Labour Party that they were going to struggle to defeat Reform in any general election.
sentences.cn: 这似乎向工党的某些部分表明，他们将在任何大选中都难以击败改革党。
sentences.structure: 主句：That seemed to suggest...；间接宾语：to certain parts of the Labour Party；宾语从句：that they were going to struggle to defeat Reform in any general election。
sentences.analysis: 'struggle to do' 意为'努力做某事'，这里表示'难以做到'；'defeat' 是'击败'。

sentences.en: There was a special election that was held in the Northwest of England last week, which he won so convincingly that it really strengthened the argument that he might be the man to defeat this right-wing populist party, Reform, in any upcoming election, since he had done it so decisively in this one constituency.
sentences.cn: 上周在英格兰西北部举行了一场特别选举，他以令人信服的方式获胜，这确实强化了一种论点，即他可能是任何即将到来的选举中击败这个右翼民粹主义政党改革党的人，因为他在这个选区如此果断地做到了这一点。
sentences.structure: 主句：There was a special election；定语从句：that was held...；非限制性定语从句：which he won...；结果状语从句：so...that...；同位语从句：the argument that...；原因状语从句：since he had done it...。
sentences.analysis: 'convincingly' 是副词，意为'令人信服地'；'strengthen the argument' 是'强化论点'；'decisively' 是'果断地'；'constituency' 是'选区'。

sentences.en: So, then, we sort of -- it all coalesced around this point.
sentences.cn: 所以，然后，我们有点——这一切都围绕这一点凝聚起来。
sentences.structure: 主句：it all coalesced around this point；插入语：we sort of。
sentences.analysis: 'sort of' 是口语化表达，意为'有点、可以说'；'coalesce around' 是固定短语，意为'围绕...凝聚'。

sentences.en: Keir Starmer spent the weekend thinking about it, addressing the sort of political realities as they stood, and made this extremely difficult decision for him to step down.
sentences.cn: 基尔·斯塔默花了整个周末思考这件事，处理当时存在的各种政治现实，并做出了这个对他来说极其艰难的下台决定。
sentences.structure: 主句：Keir Starmer spent...thinking...addressing...and made...；时间状语：as they stood。
sentences.analysis: 'spend time doing' 是固定搭配；'address' 在这里意为'处理、应对'；'as they stood' 是固定短语，意为'按照当时的情况'；'step down' 是'辞职'。

sentences.en: I will remain in post as prime minister until the contest is complete, and I will do everything I can to ensure an orderly handover of power.
sentences.cn: 我将继续担任首相职务，直到竞选结束，我将尽我所能确保权力的有序交接。
sentences.structure: 并列句：I will remain...until...and I will do...；定语从句：I can；目的状语：to ensure an orderly handover of power。
sentences.analysis: 'remain in post' 是'留任'；'contest' 这里指'领导人竞选'；'handover of power' 是'权力交接'。

sentences.en: I will also give my successor my full and unequivocal support, knowing that they will inherit a Britain that is far stronger and fairer than the one I inherited two years ago; better prepared for the challenges ahead; and better able to ensure the Labour Party secures a second term in office.
sentences.cn: 我还将给予我的继任者充分且明确的支持，因为我知道他们将继承一个比我两年前继承的更强大、更公平的英国；更好地为未来的挑战做好准备；并且更有能力确保工党赢得第二个任期。
sentences.structure: 主句：I will also give...；原因状语：knowing that...；宾语从句：they will inherit a Britain...；定语从句：that is far stronger...；比较结构：than the one I inherited...；并列结构：better prepared...and better able...。
sentences.analysis: 'unequivocal' 是'明确的'；'inherit' 是'继承'；'secure a second term' 是'赢得第二个任期'。

sentences.en: If they do, it could take a bit longer.
sentences.cn: 如果他们这样做，可能会花费更长的时间。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句：If they do；主句：it could take a bit longer。
sentences.analysis: 'do' 指代前文的'stand against Andy Burnham'；'a bit' 是程度副词，意为'一点'。

sentences.en: And pointing back to those local elections at the beginning of May, where Reform did so well.
sentences.cn: 并回溯到五月初的那些地方选举，改革党在那里表现非常好。
sentences.structure: 现在分词短语：pointing back to...；定语从句：where Reform did so well。
sentences.analysis: 'point back to' 是'回溯'；'do well' 是'表现好'。

sentences.en: There are questions about whether they've peaked after Burnham's defeat of the reform candidate in that special election.
sentences.cn: 关于他们是否在伯纳姆在那次特别选举中击败改革党候选人后已经达到顶峰，存在疑问。
sentences.structure: 主句：There are questions about...；宾语从句：whether they've peaked after...。
sentences.analysis: 'peak' 作动词，意为'达到顶峰'；'defeat of' 是'击败'。

sentences.en: And the chance now for Labour under a new leader is to try to stabilize their grip on power so they can see off Reform in a general election when it happens.
sentences.cn: 现在工党在新领导人的领导下的机会是试图稳定他们对权力的掌控，以便他们能在即将到来的大选中击败改革党。
sentences.structure: 主句：the chance...is to try to...；目的状语：so they can see off...；时间状语从句：when it happens。
sentences.analysis: 'stabilize their grip on power' 是'稳定权力掌控'；'see off' 是固定短语，意为'击败、送走'。

sentences.en: Vandalizing or attempting to vandalize the Reflecting Pool will face the criminal justice system in D.C.
sentences.cn: 破坏或试图破坏倒影池的人将面临华盛顿特区的刑事司法系统。
sentences.structure: 主句：Vandalizing or attempting to vandalize...will face...。
sentences.analysis: 动名词短语作主语；'face' 意为'面临'；'criminal justice system' 是'刑事司法系统'。

sentences.en: The president now claiming that multiple people have been arrested, accused of vandalizing the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.
sentences.cn: 总统现在声称多人被捕，被指控破坏林肯纪念堂倒影池。
sentences.structure: 主句：The president now claiming...；宾语从句：that multiple people have been arrested；过去分词短语：accused of...。
sentences.analysis: 现在分词短语作谓语；'accused of' 是'被指控'。

sentences.en: Now, this comes after his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed: the water riddled with algae, the lining ripped.
sentences.cn: 现在，这发生在他耗资1400万美元的修复项目失败之后：水中布满藻类，衬里撕裂。
sentences.structure: 主句：this comes after...；时间状语从句：his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed；冒号后为并列名词短语作补充说明。
sentences.analysis: 'comes after' 是'发生在...之后'；'rehabilitation project' 是'修复项目'；'riddled with' 是'充满'；'lining' 是'衬里'。

sentences.en: Visitors say they noticed an increased law enforcement presence following the president's post.
sentences.cn: 游客们说，他们注意到在总统发帖后，执法力量有所增加。
sentences.structure: 主句：Visitors say；宾语从句：they noticed an increased law enforcement presence；时间状语：following the president's post。
sentences.analysis: 'law enforcement presence' 是'执法力量存在'；'following' 是介词，意为'在...之后'。

sentences.en: They came up to me and two other women, asking if -- if we touched it and then just promoting us not to, that we would get arrested or charged with vandalism if we did.
sentences.cn: 他们走到我和另外两名女性面前，问我们是否碰过它，然后只是劝我们不要碰，说如果我们碰了就会被逮捕或被指控破坏公物。
sentences.structure: 主句：They came up to me and two other women；伴随状语：asking...and then promoting...；宾语从句：if we touched it；直接引语：that we would get arrested...if we did。
sentences.analysis: 'come up to' 是'走近'；'promote' 这里意为'劝告'；'charge with' 是'指控'。

topics.title: 英国首相辞职与工党领导权更迭
topics.content: 英国首相基尔·斯塔默（Keir Starmer）因工党在地方选举中失去1400多个席位、右翼改革党（Reform Party）崛起而被迫辞职。曼彻斯特市长安迪·伯纳姆（Andy Burnham）在特别选举中获胜，成为继任热门。斯塔默将留任至新领袖选出，强调有序交接。改革党领袖法拉奇（Nigel Farage）呼吁提前大选，称伯纳姆缺乏授权。
topics.keywords: resignation · local elections · Reform Party · special election · mandate

topics.title: 特朗普指控倒影池遭破坏与修复争议
topics.content: 特朗普声称林肯纪念堂倒影池（Reflecting Pool）遭人故意破坏，称已逮捕多人，但未提供证据。此前耗资1400万美元的修复工程失败，池水藻类泛滥、衬垫破裂。华盛顿特区检察官皮罗（Jeanine Pirro）表示将严惩破坏者。批评者认为问题源于工程失败，而非人为破坏。
topics.keywords: vandalism · Reflecting Pool · rehabilitation project · algae · citation

topics.title: 世界杯小国爆冷与文化融合
topics.content: 2026年世界杯上，库拉索（Curacao）等小国球队表现出色，成为赛事亮点。评论指出，赛前批评者低估了这些球队的实力，而赛场内外文化融合的场面也备受关注。
topics.keywords: World Cup · Curacao · spectacle · cultural embrace

topics.title: 美国政治动态：纽森与拜登家族互动
topics.content: 加州州长纽森（Gavin Newsom）在考虑竞选总统之际，与拜登家族互动频繁。亨特·拜登（Hunter Biden）表示愿意为纽森助选或反对其对手。分析认为这一策略可能反直觉，但有助于纽森争取民主党支持。
topics.keywords: Gavin Newsom · Hunter Biden · counterintuitive strategy · presidential campaign

quiz.question: 在 'mounting pressure to step aside' 中，'mounting' 最接近的含义是？
quiz.explanation: mounting 在此处意为'日益增加的'，与 increasing 同义。其他选项为干扰项。

quiz.question: 短语 'come out swinging' 在政治语境中通常表示？
quiz.explanation: come out swinging 是习语，意为'猛烈抨击'，常用于描述政客的激烈言论。

quiz.question: 下列哪个词最能描述 'the water riddled with algae' 中 'riddled with' 的含义？
quiz.explanation: riddled with 意为'充满（问题或不良物质）'，此处指水中满是藻类。

quiz.question: 根据报道，基尔·斯塔默辞职的直接原因是什么？
options: B. 工党在地方选举中惨败
C. 与美国总统特朗普的冲突
quiz.explanation: 报道明确指出，工党在五月地方选举中失去1400多个席位，改革党崛起，导致党内要求斯塔默下台的压力增大。

quiz.question: 关于倒影池问题，特朗普政府的主要说法是什么？
options: A. 修复工程成功，但遭人破坏
B. 自然原因导致藻类滋生
C. 资金不足导致工程停滞
D. 国家公园管理局管理不善
quiz.explanation: 特朗普声称倒影池遭人故意破坏，并称已逮捕多人，但未提供证据。报道也提到修复工程失败，但政府强调破坏行为。

quiz.question: 安迪·伯纳姆被视为工党新领袖热门的原因是什么？
options: B. 他在特别选举中赢得压倒性胜利
C. 他得到改革党支持
D. 他拥有丰富的内阁经验
quiz.explanation: 报道指出，伯纳姆在英格兰西北部的特别选举中赢得压倒性胜利，证明他能击败改革党候选人，因此成为热门人选。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.text: And it happened right before a fire tore through her home on Friday. She escaped safely with the dog, but as you can see, their house was destroyed in that fire. These are just some of the images, but excellent work, Squilliam, to make sure that you got your owners up and out. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 而这一切就发生在周五大火吞噬她的家之前。她带着狗安全逃出，但正如你所见，他们的房子在那场大火中被烧毁了。这些只是部分画面，但干得好，Squilliam，确保你的主人及时起床并逃了出来。CNN头条快讯到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥蒂·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Breaking news this morning. A political shakeup in the U.K. Keir Starmer says he is resigning.
paragraphs.cn: 今早突发新闻。英国政坛动荡。基尔·斯塔默表示他将辞职。

paragraphs.speaker: Keir Starmer
paragraphs.text: That is why I will resign as leader of the Labour Party.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是为什么我将辞去工党领袖一职。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: President Trump blames vandals for his Reflecting Pool problems. How can he be sure?
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统将倒影池的问题归咎于破坏者。他如何能确定？

paragraphs.speaker: Hunter Biden
paragraphs.text: I'll come campaign for you or against you. Whatever -- whatever helps most.
paragraphs.cn: 我会为你助选，或者反对你。无论怎样 --  -- 怎样最有帮助就怎样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Gavin Newsom embracing the Bidens as he considers running for president. Is that smart or a counterintuitive strategy? And the World Cup now a must-see spectacle both on and off the field. How did all the critics get it wrong? Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. We're going to start with this breaking news. Keir Starmer out. The British prime minister announcing his resignation in the last hour after mounting pressure to step aside.
paragraphs.cn: 加文·纽森在考虑竞选总统之际拥抱拜登家族。这是明智之举还是反直觉策略？世界杯如今成为场内外必看的盛事。所有批评者是如何搞错的？大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。我们将从这条突发新闻开始。基尔·斯塔默出局。英国首相在面临越来越大的下台压力后，于过去一小时内宣布辞职。

paragraphs.speaker: Starmer
paragraphs.text: The question my party is asking now is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election. I have heard the answer of my Parliamentary party to that question, and I accept that answer with good grace. Every decision I've taken has been about putting the country I love first. That is why I will resign as leader of the Labour Party.
paragraphs.cn: 我的政党现在提出的问题是，我是否是最适合带领我们进入下一次大选的人。我已经听到了议会党团对这个问题的回答，我欣然接受这个回答。我所做的每一个决定都是为了把我热爱的国家放在首位。这就是为什么我将辞去工党领袖一职。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We're going to go now to CNN's Clare Sebastian in London. Clare, very busy day there. Can you start by talking about why now? I know it seems inevitable, but what's the sense of why Keir Starmer stepped down?
paragraphs.cn: 我们现在连线CNN驻伦敦的克莱尔·塞巴斯蒂安。克莱尔，今天那里非常忙碌。你能先谈谈为什么是现在吗？我知道这似乎不可避免，但基尔·斯塔默下台的原因是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.text: So, look, the clinching factor for this, Audie, after months of difficulties that the prime minister has faced for a number of different reasons. But the real clinching factor was that we had local elections across the U.K. in various parts in May, and the Labour Party suffered a massive defeat, losing more than 1,400 local council seats, even as we saw the right-wing populist Reform Party, led by Nigel Farage, surge. So, that was a real wake-up call.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，奥蒂，决定性因素在于，首相数月来因多种原因面临困境。但真正的决定性因素是，我们在5月举行了英国各地的地方选举，工党遭遇惨败，失去了超过1400个地方议会席位，而与此同时，奈杰尔·法拉奇领导的右翼民粹主义改革党却异军突起。所以，这真是一记警钟。

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.text: That seemed to suggest to certain parts of the Labour Party that they were going to struggle to defeat Reform in any general election. We have to have a general election in this country by around the middle of 2029. So, that is when the calls for Prime Minister Starmer to go grew even louder.
paragraphs.cn: 这似乎向工党某些部分表明，他们在任何大选中都难以击败改革党。英国必须在2029年中左右举行大选。因此，要求首相斯塔默下台的呼声更加高涨。

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.text: But on the flip side, we also have this issue where Andy Burnham, who was then the mayor of Manchester, was seen as the most popular politician within that party, but he was not a member of Parliament, so couldn't officially challenge the prime minister. So, then this process started to try and get him back into Parliament.
paragraphs.cn: 但另一方面，我们还有一个问题：时任曼彻斯特市长安迪·伯纳姆被视为党内最受欢迎的政治人物，但他不是议会成员，因此无法正式挑战首相。于是，他们开始设法让他重返议会。

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.text: There was a special election that was held in the Northwest of England last week, which he won so convincingly that it really strengthened the argument that he might be the man to defeat this right-wing populist party, Reform, in any upcoming election, since he had done it so decisively in this one constituency. So, then, we sort of -- it all coalesced around this point.
paragraphs.cn: 上周在英格兰西北部举行了一场特别选举，他以压倒性优势获胜，这极大地强化了一个论点：他可能是那个在即将到来的任何选举中击败右翼民粹主义政党"改革党"的人，因为他已经在这个选区果断地做到了。于是，所有焦点都集中到了这一点上。

paragraphs.speaker: Clare Sebastian
paragraphs.text: Keir Starmer spent the weekend thinking about it, addressing the sort of political realities as they stood, and made this extremely difficult decision for him to step down. So, now we have a transition. Take a listen to the prime minister talking about how that will play out.
paragraphs.cn: 基尔·斯塔默花了整个周末思考这个问题，面对当时的政治现实，做出了极其艰难的决定 --  -- 辞职。所以，现在我们正处于过渡期。听听首相谈论这将如何展开。

paragraphs.speaker: Starmer
paragraphs.text: I will remain in post as prime minister until the contest is complete, and I will do everything I can to ensure an orderly handover of power. I will also give my successor my full and unequivocal support, knowing that they will inherit a Britain that is far stronger and fairer than the one I inherited two years ago; better prepared for the challenges ahead; and better able to ensure the Labour Party secures a second term in office.
paragraphs.cn: 在竞选结束前，我将继续担任首相，并尽一切努力确保权力有序交接。我还将全力、明确地支持我的继任者，因为他们将继承一个比我两年前接手时更强大、更公平的英国；更好地应对未来的挑战；并更有能力确保工党赢得第二个任期。

paragraphs.speaker: Sebastian
paragraphs.text: So, the question now is, will there be a contest? Will anyone else stand against Andy Burnham? If they do, it could take a bit longer. If they don't, this could all be over in a couple of weeks -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，现在的问题是：会有竞争吗？会有人与安迪·伯纳姆竞争吗？如果有人参选，过程可能会更长。如果没有，这一切可能在几周内结束 --  -- 奥迪。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Right. And Burnham, this will be basically third time's the charm, because he has tried to be Labour leader before. Trump ally Nigel Farage actually speaking out after the news of this resignation. Do they feel like this is an opportunity for the Reform Party?
paragraphs.cn: 没错。对伯纳姆来说，这基本上是第三次尝试才成功，因为他之前曾两次竞选工党领袖。特朗普的盟友奈杰尔·法拉奇在辞职消息传出后发表了言论。他们是否认为这对改革党来说是一个机会？

paragraphs.speaker: Sebastian
paragraphs.text: Look, they suffered a really big defeat in this special election last week. So, Farage has come out swinging today, calling in a post on X, for a general election and making the point in an essay that Andy Burnham doesn't -- having not been officially elected in a general election -- have a mandate to lead. And pointing back to those local elections at the beginning of May, where Reform did so well. I think look, it is a moment for reform to try to assert themselves.
paragraphs.cn: 听着，他们在上周的特别选举中遭遇了重大失败。所以法拉奇今天猛烈反击，在X上发帖呼吁举行大选，并在文章中指出安迪·伯纳姆 --  -- 由于未在大选中正式当选 --  -- 没有领导授权。他还提到了五月初的地方选举，改革党在那次选举中表现很好。我认为，这是改革党试图彰显自身存在的时刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Sebastian
paragraphs.text: There are questions about whether they've peaked after Burnham's defeat of the reform candidate in that special election. But as of now, in, you know, opinion polls based on voter intentions for right now, they are leading in the country. So, they are going to be doing everything they can to keep up the momentum. And, of course, the chance now for Labour under a new leader is to try to stabilize their grip on power so they can see off Reform in a general election when it happens -- Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 有人质疑，在伯纳姆于特别选举中击败改革党候选人后，改革党是否已经达到顶峰。但就目前而言，根据选民意向的民调，他们在全国领先。因此，他们将竭尽全力保持势头。当然，现在工党在新领袖领导下的机会是，努力稳定对权力的掌控，以便在大选来临时击败改革党 --  -- 奥迪。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's Clare Sebastian in London, where U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has stepped down. And coming up on CNN THIS MORNING from: reflection to outrage. Arrests over the algae-plagued Reflecting Pool and the rising costs. Critics are calling it all money down the drain. Plus, are world leaders losing patience with President Trump? Italy's prime minister is the latest. And little countries doing big things at the FIFA World Cup.
paragraphs.cn: 以上是克莱尔·塞巴斯蒂安在伦敦的报道，英国首相基尔·斯塔默已辞职。接下来在CNN《今晨》节目中：从反思到愤怒。因藻类泛滥的反思池被捕及成本上升。批评者称这一切都是打水漂。此外，世界领导人是否对特朗普总统失去耐心？意大利总理是最新一例。还有，小国家在世界杯上大展身手。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Curacao came to conquer, guys. I'm telling you this once. I'm telling you this twice. Curacao came to show up. Let's go.
paragraphs.cn: 库拉索来征服了，伙计们。我跟你说一次，我跟你说两次。库拉索来展示了。出发吧。

paragraphs.speaker: Jeanine Pirro
paragraphs.text: Vandalizing or attempting to vandalize the Reflecting Pool will face the criminal justice system in
paragraphs.cn: 破坏或试图破坏倒影池将面临刑事司法系统。

paragraphs.speaker: D.C. Cornish
paragraphs.text: Vandalism or something else? President Trump says he knows what is causing the problems at the Reflecting Pool. The president now claiming that multiple people have been arrested, accused of vandalizing the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. He has not provided evidence to back up that claim. A senior administration official is saying that police arrested five people for vandalism, that they issued citations to five others. Now, Trump did say work will begin immediately to fix it.
paragraphs.cn: 破坏行为还是其他原因？特朗普总统称他知道倒影池问题的根源。总统现在声称，多人因涉嫌破坏林肯纪念堂倒影池被捕。他并未提供证据支持这一说法。一位高级行政官员表示，警方因破坏行为逮捕了五人，并向另外五人发出了传票。特朗普表示，修复工作将立即开始。

paragraphs.speaker: D.C. Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, this comes after his $14 million rehabilitation project has failed: the water riddled with algae, the lining ripped. Visitors say they noticed an increased law enforcement presence following the president's post.
paragraphs.cn: 此前，他耗资1400万美元的修复项目失败：池水布满藻类，衬垫撕裂。游客称，在总统发文后，他们注意到执法力量有所增加。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: I've never seen this many National Guards in the National Park area. They came up to me and two other women, asking if -- if we touched it and then just promoting us not to, that we would get arrested or charged with vandalism if we did.
paragraphs.cn: 我从未在国家公园区域见过这么多国民警卫队。他们走向我和另外两名女性，询问我们是否触碰了池水，并警告我们不要这样做，否则会被逮捕或被指控破坏公物。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat, Eleanor Mueller, White House economic policy reporter with "Semafor"; Julia Manchester, national political reporter for "The Hill"; and Elena Schneider, national political reporter at "NOTUS." Where to begin? Where to begin? Is vandalism the problem, or the green water we just showed you coming out of a hose? I want to play -- the reason why I'm talking about this is Jeanine Pirro, U.S. attorney for D.C., jumped in on this vandalization thing. I don't know if we have that clip.
paragraphs.cn: 今天参与群聊的有："Semafor"白宫经济政策记者埃莉诺·穆勒；"The Hill"全国政治记者朱莉娅·曼彻斯特；以及"NOTUS"全国政治记者埃琳娜·施奈德。从何说起？破坏行为是问题所在，还是我们刚才看到的从水管里流出的绿水？我想播放 --  -- 我之所以谈论这个，是因为华盛顿特区联邦检察官珍妮·皮尔罗也介入了这起破坏事件。我不知道我们是否有那段视频。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But she was asked about this, and I want to play for you what she had to say.
paragraphs.cn: 但她被问及此事，我想播放她的回应。

paragraphs.speaker: Pirro
paragraphs.text: There are several citations that have been handed out to individuals. And these are cases that will be prosecuted to the full extent. If there are more serious products that are put into the Reflecting Pool to create more algae or a bigger problem, then we'll consider more serious charges.
paragraphs.cn: 已经向多人发出了传票。这些案件将受到全面起诉。如果有人向倒影池投放更严重的物质以制造更多藻类或更大问题，我们将考虑更严重的指控。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Help. JULIA MANCHESTER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "THE HILL": You know, it's interesting that this came after we saw a video of hydrogen peroxide being poured into the Reflecting Pool. I'm not a scientist. However. you know, I think it's just interesting to see the sequence of events. Certainly frustrating for the president.
paragraphs.cn: 救命。朱莉娅·曼彻斯特（"The Hill"全国政治记者）：有趣的是，这发生在我们看到有人向倒影池倒入过氧化氢的视频之后。我不是科学家。但我觉得事件顺序很有意思。总统肯定很沮丧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We should say, the people pouring those jugs of hydrogen peroxide --
paragraphs.cn: 需要说明的是，那些倒入过氧化氢罐子的人 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- were government workers trying to --
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 是试图修复问题的政府工作人员。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- repair the work of these contractors that the president invited through no insistence of Congress. Literally, no one asked for this.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 修复这些承包商的工作，是总统在没有国会坚持要求的情况下邀请的。说实话，没人要求这么做。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And now here we are.
paragraphs.cn: 而现在我们就在这里。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: And it's frustrating, I think, for the president, because he's put so much stock, I think, publicly, politically --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为，这让总统感到沮丧，因为他投入了太多，无论是在公开场合还是政治上 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Frustrating for him.
paragraphs.cn: 这让他感到沮丧。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: And yes, for everyone.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，也让所有人感到沮丧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Let me play for you one of these tourists. OK? People are walking by, not vandalizing things, though clearly threatened if they are. And here's an example of just someone by the Reflecting Pool, kind of what they see.
paragraphs.cn: 让我给你放一段游客的录音。好吗？人们走过，没有破坏东西，尽管如果他们这样做会明显受到威胁。这是一个例子，有人在倒影池旁，看看他们看到了什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: So, what do you think?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，你觉得怎么样？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Oh, it's embarrassing. Yes, that's --
paragraphs.cn: 哦，这很尴尬。是的，那是 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Yes. Money well spent.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。钱花得值。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: Well, it's a little dirty. So, yes. Just a little.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，有点脏。所以，是的。只是有点脏。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Can you even see the blue?
paragraphs.cn: 你甚至能看到蓝色吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: No, all I see is greens.
paragraphs.cn: 不，我只能看到绿色。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Out of the mouths of babes. The cost of the Reflecting Pool so far. Originally, we were told it would be 1.5 million. That's not what it is now. Now it is upwards of 14.8 million of green water. ELENA SCHNEIDER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": And it was also supposed to be done within two weeks. It's obviously not done within two weeks. So, look, I mean, this was a no-bid contract that was given to a pool company that had connections to the Trump administration.
paragraphs.cn: 童言无忌。倒影池目前的成本。最初，我们被告知是150万美元。但现在不是这样了。现在它高达1480万美元的绿水。埃琳娜·施奈德，国家政治记者，"NOTUS"：而且它本应在两周内完成。显然没有在两周内完成。所以，你看，这是一份无竞标合同，给了一家与特朗普政府有联系的泳池公司。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: "The New York Times" has done some great reporting on -- on sort of the questions surrounding who they hired and brought on to do this kind of work. But look, we know what's at the heart of this, which is that Donald Trump cares a lot about the way that Washington, D.C., looks in this moment, particularly as it comes to the 250th anniversary just a couple of weeks away. There is something that he really cares is something that he really cares about, the way that the city looks.
paragraphs.cn: 《纽约时报》做了一些很好的报道，关于他们雇佣谁来从事这类工作的疑问。但你看，我们知道问题的核心是什么，那就是唐纳德·特朗普非常关心华盛顿特区此刻的样子，尤其是距离250周年纪念只有几周了。他真正关心的是这座城市的外观。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And one can argue that there are some fountains that are now running in places like Malcolm X Park or outside Union Station, which it is nice to have fountains.
paragraphs.cn: 有人可能会说，现在有些喷泉在马尔科姆·X公园或联合车站外运行，有喷泉是件好事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I know, but this is six acres.
paragraphs.cn: 我知道，但这是六英亩。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And telling people that this public property, which belongs to us, the American people, we can't touch it now, because we are the ones who have vandalized it, I feel like is delusional. ELEANOR MUELLER, WHITE HOUSE ECONOMIC POLICY REPORTER, "SEMAFOR": One of those arrested was a three-time Olympian canoeist who actually gave interviews afterwards, saying that he just bent down. He'd reached into the water. He touched the bottom of the pool.
paragraphs.cn: 告诉人们这块属于我们美国人民的公共财产，我们现在不能碰它，因为是我们破坏了它，我觉得这很荒谬。埃莉诺·米勒，白宫经济政策记者，"SEMAFOR"：其中一名被捕者是一名三届奥运皮划艇运动员，他事后接受采访说，他只是弯下腰，把手伸进水里，碰到了池底。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And then when he walked away is when National Park Service federal officers had approached him and said that they were arresting him for vandalism. And so, I'm --
paragraphs.cn: 然后当他走开时，国家公园管理局的联邦官员走近他，说他们以破坏公物罪逮捕他。所以，我 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Like, do you have gold medals? Don't care.
paragraphs.cn: 比如，你有金牌吗？不在乎。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now's the time for your criminal origin story to begin.
paragraphs.cn: 现在是你犯罪起源故事开始的时候了。

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.text: And I think it says a lot that Jeanine Pirro is the one leading the charge because she's shown herself, again and again, to be the person who's willing to take on Trump's sometimes more political, not to say vendettas, but certainly, agendas against people. She was the -- obviously, same prosecutor who led the investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell over the --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为，珍妮·皮罗带头冲锋这一点很能说明问题，因为她一次又一次地证明了自己是愿意承担特朗普有时更具政治性 --  -- 且不说报复 --  -- 但肯定是针对他人的议程的人。她显然是同一位检察官，曾领导对美联储主席杰罗姆·鲍威尔就 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.text: -- renovation of the Fed's headquarters.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 美联储总部翻新工程的调查。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, we're bringing to bear the power of the state, the legal law enforcement arm --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我们正在动用州的力量，即法律执法部门 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- of the district to solve a public relations problem. That's what it looks like on its surface.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 来解决一个公关问题。从表面上看就是这样。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: Yes. I mean, look, I think anyone who lives in Washington, D.C., agrees that, probably, this is not the biggest issue that this city faces right now is, you know, quote, vandalism of people touching the Reflecting Pool. But Jeanine Pirro, like many people in Trump's cabinet, understands that they have an audience of one, and they're going to serve that audience.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我的意思是，我认为任何住在华盛顿特区的人都会同意，这可能不是这座城市目前面临的最大问题，即所谓的"破坏公物" --  -- 人们触摸倒影池。但珍妮·皮罗，像特朗普内阁中的许多人一样，明白他们只有一个观众，而他们将服务于那个观众。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. You guys, thank you so much. I want you to stay with us as we talk to you. Said more interesting things today. More important things today, like this. Game, set, Serena. Biggest thing in my group chats: the tennis icon will return to singles play at Wimbledon. Plus, the World Cup isn't just about soccer. It's about the vibes, and the cultures coming together are vibing. Good morning to everyone, in the meantime, from the Philadelphia Stadium, where France and Iraq will face off in a game today.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。非常感谢你们。我希望你们继续和我们在一起，我们正在和你们交谈。今天说了更多有趣的事情。今天更重要的事情，比如这个。比赛结束，塞雷娜。我的群聊里最大的消息：这位网球偶像将重返温布尔登单打赛场。另外，世界杯不仅仅是足球。它是关于氛围，文化融合在一起产生的共鸣。与此同时，来自费城体育场的大家早上好，法国和伊拉克今天将在这里进行一场比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Many people probably wrote us off or didn't know much about us, and that was sort of our motivation, as well. Let's show the world that we -- we deserve to compete.
paragraphs.cn: 很多人可能认为我们不行，或者对我们了解不多，这也是我们的动力之一。让我们向世界展示我们 --  -- 我们值得竞争。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Cape Verde and their fairy tale continues. The tiny island nation stunned Spain last week, and they just did it again, ending in a 2-2 draw against Uruguay on Sunday. And they got their first goal in the World Cup, one step closer to a legitimate chance of getting into the knockout stage. So, you've got dreams coming true. That's what the World Cup is all about. And the worries about filling stadiums and selling tickets at this point are gone because of moments like these.
paragraphs.cn: 佛得角，他们的童话还在继续。这个小小的岛国上周震惊了西班牙，而他们刚刚再次做到了，周日以2-2战平乌拉圭。他们打进了世界杯的第一个进球，离真正有机会进入淘汰赛阶段更近了一步。所以，梦想成真了。这就是世界杯的意义所在。而关于填满体育场和售票的担忧，在这样时刻已经烟消云散。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Whether we're watching the next greats score their first World Cup goal or -- of what should be many to come, or seeing a team claim a World Cup victory for the first time in their history. Perhaps one of the most uniting things: seeing different cultures embracing.
paragraphs.cn: 无论我们是在观看下一位伟大球员打进他们的第一个世界杯进球 --  -- 这应该是众多进球中的第一个，还是看到一支球队历史上首次赢得世界杯胜利。也许最团结人心的事情之一：看到不同文化的融合。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: As you pass statues in Boston, you're seeing the Tartan Army puts traffic cones on statues over in Scotland, and they brought that tradition back to Massachusetts.
paragraphs.cn: 当你在波士顿经过雕像时，你会看到苏格兰的"格子呢军团"给雕像戴上交通锥，他们把这一传统带回了马萨诸塞州。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: That's your first ever bite of Texas barbecue. What do you think?
paragraphs.cn: 这是你第一次品尝得克萨斯烤肉。你觉得怎么样？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I don't necessarily know if the United States was the best place for the World Cup, but I do feel like it is a very important place for this to be happening because of the amount of healing that is probably happening to our society.
paragraphs.cn: 我不确定美国是否是举办世界杯的最佳地点，但我确实觉得这是一个非常重要的地方，因为这对我们的社会可能起到很大的疗愈作用。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Joining me now, Will Leitch, contributing editor for "New York Magazine" sports. Nature is healing is what I'm hearing. Why do you think all of this, like, culture clash is such a big deal? And I especially hear it coming out of the South, I should say: the fans from abroad who are visiting there. WILL LEITCH, CONTRIBUTING EDITOR, "NEW YORK MAGAZINE": Yes, to hear the people from the Netherlands talk about their Kansas City barbecue has really been kind of a wonderful aspect of this, too. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。现在请到的是《纽约杂志》体育版特约编辑威尔·莱奇。我听到的是"自然在疗愈"。为什么你认为所有这些文化冲突如此重要？我特别注意到来自南方的声音：那些来自国外的球迷正在那里访问。《纽约杂志》特约编辑威尔·莱奇：是的，听到荷兰人谈论他们的堪萨斯城烤肉，这确实是这件事的一个美妙方面。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You know, remember this was a -- we've talked about this. People were very concerned about coming in -- coming to the United States for a variety of reasons, from immigration to ICE to -- and there have been issues. The Iranian team, in particular, has been treated pretty shabbily by the U.S. government in this. But generally speaking, this tournament has gone off kind of magnificently in a way that has felt pretty healing for the United States at a certain level. You know, it's been a good reminder.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，记得我们之前讨论过。人们出于各种原因非常担心来到美国，从移民到移民和海关执法局 --  -- 而且确实存在问题。特别是伊朗队，在这件事上受到了美国政府相当糟糕的对待。但总的来说，这次赛事进行得非常精彩，在某种程度上对美国起到了疗愈作用。你知道，这是一个很好的提醒。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: You know, we're kind of so caught up in the morass of everything that we do have a lot to show people, a lot of things to be proud of. I think you've seen that this is what the World Cup does, right? All of those worries about the World Cup, what's going to happen with this? What's going to happen with that? Once the game gets started, the games themselves are wonderful, but also all of these different cultures. I was fortunate to be at the Spain-Cabo Verde game, that first game in Atlanta.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，我们有点陷入各种事情的泥潭，但我们确实有很多东西可以向人们展示，有很多值得骄傲的事情。我想你已经看到这就是世界杯的作用，对吧？所有关于世界杯的担忧，这个会发生什么？那个会发生什么？一旦比赛开始，比赛本身就很精彩，而且还有所有这些不同的文化。我有幸参加了西班牙对佛得角的比赛，那是亚特兰大的第一场比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And just to -- to celebrate with Cabo Verde fans after. Cabo Verde has fewer people than Mesa, Arizona. I mean, this is a very, very small country. And the joy that they have, that you see this at every single one of these places. These venues have been full. The tickets have been very, very expensive. But the seats are full; the stands are full. So, people are going out to these games and really kind of having a pretty wonderful time.
paragraphs.cn: 之后和佛得角球迷一起庆祝。佛得角的人口比亚利桑那州的梅萨还少。我的意思是，这是一个非常非常小的国家。他们的喜悦，你在每一个地方都能看到。这些场馆都坐满了。门票非常非常贵。但座位都满了；看台都满了。所以，人们都去看这些比赛，并且真的玩得很开心。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We talked about Cape Verde, as you mentioned, but we've got to talk about the U.S. Elmo weighed in online. He didn't want to step in it again, as he did with the Knicks. So, he actually, I think, tweeted, "Just to be clear, USA to win. OK? Elmo loves everybody. Just to be clear, don't make this a thing." Honestly, evergreen tweet. But can you talk about how the U.S. is doing?
paragraphs.cn: 我们谈到了佛得角，正如你提到的，但我们还得谈谈美国。艾摩在网上发表了看法。他不想再像对尼克斯队那样踩雷。所以，他实际上，我想，发推文说："说清楚，美国队赢。好吗？艾摩爱大家。说清楚，别搞事情。"老实说，这是永恒的推文。但你能谈谈美国队表现如何吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: We live in an age of Elmo having to say, "Don't make this a thing."
paragraphs.cn: 我们生活在一个艾摩不得不说"别搞事情"的时代。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: As a general rule. But yet, the U.S. team has really -- this is the best team they've ever had in the World Cup. They finished third in 1930. It was a much smaller field and a much different game. The U.S. have looked terrific in a lot of ways, especially Pochettino, their coach, is a big -- He's coached Tottenham Hotspurs to the -- to the Champions League final. He was brought in to kind of fix this team. It has worked. They have played terrific.
paragraphs.cn: 但总的来说，美国队确实 --  -- 这是他们在世界杯上最好的队伍。他们在1930年获得第三名。当时参赛队伍少得多，比赛也大不相同。美国队在很多方面看起来都很棒，尤其是他们的教练波切蒂诺，他很重要 --  -- 他曾带领托特纳姆热刺队进入欧冠决赛。他被请来是为了修复这支队伍。这奏效了。他们踢得非常出色。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: They haven't played any fantastic teams yet, but they've looked organized. They've played great. They've already won their group. We already know when their first game in the knockout round is going to be. It's going to be July 1, and -- it's going to be July 1. And so, it should set up very, very well for them. They have looked exciting. Generally speaking, the U.S., because their talent level has been lower, they've always played kind of a defensive game. They've always kind of quiet, good.
paragraphs.cn: 他们还没有遇到任何非常强的队伍，但他们看起来组织有序。他们踢得很好。他们已经赢得了小组赛。我们已经知道他们淘汰赛的第一场比赛将在7月1日进行。所以，这对他们来说非常有利。他们看起来令人兴奋。总的来说，美国队因为天赋水平较低，一直踢的是防守型比赛。他们一直比较安静，表现不错。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: They've had good keepers and, like, tried to kind of just like fend off attacks. That's not what this team is doing. This team is playing hard. This team is playing fast, and it's scoring a lot of goals. It is very inspiring. You talk about the ticket prices. Initially, they were worries that the U.S. game against Paraguay was not going to sell out.
paragraphs.cn: 他们有过优秀的守门员，也曾试图抵挡进攻。但这支球队不是这样做的。这支球队拼得很凶，打得很快，而且进了很多球。这非常鼓舞人心。说到票价，最初人们担心美国队对阵巴拉圭的比赛会卖不完。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: That's not an issue for any of the rest of these games. There are tickets going for -- the given price for the -- for their first knockout game is now, I think, up to $4,000. They're -- people are very excited about this team in a way that is very new for the U.S. team. Usually, it's just a -- don't embarrass us. Don't -- don't make us look bad on a stage. They're not to the women's level yet, to be very clear.
paragraphs.cn: 但其余比赛都不存在这个问题。门票价格 --  -- 他们第一场淘汰赛的票价现在，我想，已经涨到4000美元了。人们对这支球队非常兴奋，这对美国队来说是全新的。通常，只是别让我们难堪。别让我们在舞台上丢脸。但明确地说，他们还没有达到女足的水平。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: The women's are a world power. The U.S. is not there yet, but this is certainly the best men's team they've had in a very, very long time.
paragraphs.cn: 女足是世界强队。美国男足还没到那个水平，但这绝对是他们很长很长时间以来最好的男足队伍。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: No, that's a great reminder. You know, I want to mention this. Iran's team actually left a note in the locker room in L.A. I just want to show people on this little notebook here. It says, "From the ancient Persia of thousands of years ago to the civilized Iran of today, the spirit of Iran remains alive and steadfast. We came to Los Angeles with pride, competed with honor, and leave with dignity. Thank you, Los Angeles, for your hospitality." We weren't sure they'd be able to be here or leave a note like that.
paragraphs.cn: 不，这是个很好的提醒。我想提一下。伊朗队实际上在洛杉矶的更衣室里留下了一张纸条。我想给大家看看这个小笔记本。上面写着："从数千年前的古老波斯到如今文明的伊朗，伊朗的精神依然鲜活而坚定。我们带着自豪来到洛杉矶，带着荣誉参赛，带着尊严离开。感谢洛杉矶的热情款待。"我们之前不确定他们能否来到这里或留下这样的纸条。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And it reminds us of what's going on politically. Is there another shoe to drop? Is Trump going to make an appear after FIFA gave him a very special peace adjacent prize?
paragraphs.cn: 这让我们想起了政治局势。是否还有后续？特朗普在国际足联授予他一个非常特殊的和平相关奖项后，会现身吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: Yes, very special prize. Yes. So, I think that the -- the final is in MetLife Stadium -- or sorry, New York-New Jersey Stadium in -- in East Rutherford. So, it's very possible there could be a Knicks-level-esque appearance for him. Certainly, he's been a big -- he's been a big part. He's been distracted by the pool lately. But generally speaking, he'd been a big part of what was going on with -- with this World Cup. He was at the draw, famously got his -- got his peace prize.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，非常特殊的奖项。是的。所以，我认为决赛将在MetLife体育场 --  -- 或者抱歉，是在东卢瑟福的纽约-新泽西体育场举行。所以，他很有可能像尼克斯队那样亮相。当然，他一直是个重要角色。最近他因为泳池分心了。但总的来说，他一直是这届世界杯的重要参与者。他出席了抽签仪式，还 famously 获得了他的和平奖。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: And I feel like, for all the worries that like, listen, ICE has not been a major presence in this tournament, like a lot of people worried about. The Iranian team, in particular, has been treated incredibly shabbily in this tournament. They've had to fly out after their games; have not been allowed to stay in the country. They've had to leave, actually, the night of the game to go back to Mexico. Just -- just this weekend U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 我觉得，尽管人们担心移民和海关执法局在这届赛事中不会像很多人担心的那样成为主要存在，但伊朗队尤其受到了非常糟糕的待遇。他们每场比赛后都必须飞离；不允许留在美国。实际上，他们必须在比赛当晚离开，返回墨西哥。就在这个周末，美国

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: Homeland Secretary Markwayne Mullin claimed that a member of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps was about to get on their plane. The team denies this, says this is in no way true. It -- this -- of all the teams in this, the one team that I think is actually a pretty inspiring story is Iran, in addition to the U.S. team, for the conditions that they've had to play under and have generally succeeded. They've played very well in this tournament.
paragraphs.cn: 国土安全部长马克韦恩·穆林声称，一名伊斯兰革命卫队成员即将登上他们的飞机。伊朗队否认这一点，称这绝非事实。在所有参赛队伍中，我认为伊朗队除了美国队之外，实际上是一个相当鼓舞人心的故事，考虑到他们必须比赛的条件，并且总体上取得了成功。他们在本届赛事中表现非常出色。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: There is a non-zero possibility Iran certainly looks like they could advance to the knockout round and keep going. We -- this has been a very -- generally, other than the Iranian team, most of the major fears that people have worried about have not happened. But we still have a long way to go in this tournament. And eventually, the pool may be blue, and there may be something for -- for Trump to -- to turn his attention back to this.
paragraphs.cn: 伊朗队看起来很有可能晋级淘汰赛并继续前进。这届赛事除了伊朗队之外，人们担心的大部分主要问题都没有发生。但比赛还有很长的路要走。最终，泳池可能会变蓝，特朗普可能会把注意力转回这里。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.text: But so far, it's really been -- the focus has remained with the games and on the international spirit that the U.S. has been able to welcome here, making the Iranian team, even if the government isn't making them feel welcome, the fans are. And I think that speaks to kind of the really wonderful thing about this tournament so far, and really kind of every four years.
paragraphs.cn: 但到目前为止，焦点一直停留在比赛上，以及美国能够在此欢迎的国际精神上，即使政府没有让伊朗队感到受欢迎，球迷们却做到了。我认为这体现了本届赛事以及每四年一次的这种美妙之处。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, Will Leitch, I know you're in Georgia, and there's like eight games that are going to be there. So, I'm sure you're going to be having a blast. Will Leitch, contributing writer at "New York Magazine." Thank you.
paragraphs.cn: 威尔·莱奇，我知道你在佐治亚州，那里大概有八场比赛。所以，我相信你会玩得很开心。威尔·莱奇，《纽约杂志》特约撰稿人。谢谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to get back to this breaking news. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announcing his resignation. Plus, this here at home: a rash of tornadoes tear through the Midwest. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 现在，在CNN今晨节目中，我们将回到这条突发新闻。英国首相基尔·斯塔默宣布辞职。此外，国内消息：一连串龙卷风席卷中西部。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. Thank you for joining me on CNN THIS MORNING. It is now half past the hour. And here's what's happening right now. At least two people are dead after a tornado outbreak in the Mideast. More -- in the Midwest. More than three dozen tornado reports were logged in the region by the storm prediction center. The deaths happened at two separate mobile homes in rural Illinois yesterday. Now that storm system is moving into the mid-Atlantic.
paragraphs.cn: 大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什。感谢您收看CNN今晨节目。现在是半小时过一点。以下是正在发生的事情。中西部地区龙卷风爆发，至少两人死亡。风暴预测中心在该地区记录了超过36份龙卷风报告。死亡事件发生在昨天伊利诺伊州农村的两处移动房屋中。现在，该风暴系统正在向中大西洋地区移动。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: And the DEA reportedly allowed hundreds of thousands of fentanyl pills to circulate in New Mexico between 2023 and 2025. That's according to three current and former DEA agents who spoke with the "Associated Press." It was a controversial tactic aimed at building larger criminal cases against drug traffickers. They claim, quote, "we 100 percent got people killed. And this morning, Vice President J.D. Vance is in Switzerland for high-level diplomatic talks with Iranian officials.
paragraphs.cn: 据报道，美国缉毒局在2023年至2025年间允许数十万颗芬太尼药片在新墨西哥州流通。这是根据三位现任和前任DEA特工向美联社透露的消息。这是一种旨在针对毒贩建立更大刑事案件的有争议策略。他们声称，"我们百分之百导致了人员死亡。"今天上午，副总统J.D.万斯在瑞士与伊朗官员进行高级别外交谈判。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Sunday the two sides held marathon talks to try to establish a long term peace agreement with Iran. But the talks so far have been described as positive and constructive according to mediators from Pakistan and Qatar. And this morning, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, resigning.
paragraphs.cn: 周日，双方进行了马拉松式会谈，试图与伊朗达成长期和平协议。但据巴基斯坦和卡塔尔的调解人称，到目前为止，会谈被描述为积极且富有建设性。今天上午，英国首相基尔·斯塔默辞职。

paragraphs.speaker: Keir Starmer
paragraphs.text: The question my party is asking now is whether I am best placed to lead us into the next general election. I have heard the answer of my parliamentary party to that question. And I accept that answer with good grace.
paragraphs.cn: 我的政党现在问的问题是，我是否是最适合带领我们进入下一次大选的人。我已经听到了我的议会党团对这个问题的回答。我欣然接受这个回答。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, nominations to select a new Labour Party leader are set to open early next month. Starmer says he hopes a new prime minister will be in place when parliament returns from summer recess. That's in September. Starmer's resignation comes just shy of two years into his tenure. Joining me now to discuss, Patrick Baker, host of "Politico's" "Westminster Insider" podcast. Good morning. Thank you so much for being here. PATRICK BAKER, HOST, "POLITICO'S" "WESTMINSTER INSIDER"
paragraphs.cn: 现在，选举新工党领袖的提名将于下月初开始。斯塔默表示，他希望议会夏季休会结束后，也就是九月，能有一位新首相就位。斯塔默辞职时，他的任期刚满两年还差一点。现在加入我讨论的是帕特里克·贝克，《政治报》的"威斯敏斯特内幕"播客主持人。早上好。非常感谢你的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Podcast
paragraphs.text: Hi. Great to be with you.
paragraphs.cn: 你好。很高兴和你在一起。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, I just want to remind people, there's been almost half a dozen prime ministers since Brexit, and it's been a decade since Brexit. So, I think, to the American public, we see a lot of churn. What was it that took Starmer down in the end?
paragraphs.cn: 现在，我想提醒大家，自英国脱欧以来，已经换了近六位首相，而英国脱欧已经过去十年了。所以，我想，对美国公众来说，我们看到很多变动。最终是什么导致了斯塔默的下台？

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: I think when Starmer got elected two years ago on this mandate of change, that was the Labour Party's slogan into the last election almost exactly two years ago, there was a real desire and a real hope that they would be different to the conservative party that had gone before, who had, as you say, had run through a whole host of different prime ministers.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为，当斯塔默两年前以变革的使命当选时 --  -- 这几乎是两年前工党在上次大选中的口号 --  -- 人们确实渴望并希望他们会与之前的保守党不同，正如你所说，保守党已经经历了一连串不同的首相。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: Keir Starmer's pitch was, I'm the man to bring stability and order to British politics, but they basically made a series of what felt like unforced errors right at the start, which really set the tone. They decided to cut winter fuel allowance for elderly people in a move that was kind of seen as really typically un- Labour. And also there was a scandal over Keir Starmer accepting free gifts as prime minister, like a suit, glasses, those kind of things.
paragraphs.cn: 基尔·斯塔默的竞选口号是，我是那个能为英国政治带来稳定和秩序的人，但他们一开始就犯了一系列看似非受迫性失误，这确实定下了基调。他们决定削减老年人的冬季燃料补贴，这一举动被视为非常不符合工党传统。此外，还有一起丑闻，涉及基尔·斯塔默作为首相接受免费礼物，比如西装、眼镜之类的东西。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: Kind of sounds like small things, but this really at the start of those two years, set the framing, set the tone for people to kind of start to wonder, actually, is this government very different to what we've seen before? Now, obviously, as it's developed, we've seen more recently there's been a real scandal over the appointment of Peter Mandelson, the ambassador to the United States.
paragraphs.cn: 听起来像是小事，但这在最初两年里确实设定了框架和基调，让人们开始怀疑，这个政府真的和我们以前见过的有很大不同吗？现在，显然，随着事态发展，我们最近看到彼得·曼德尔森被任命为驻美国大使引发了一场真正的丑闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: And his judgment has really been called into question over that, as we found out more and more through the constant release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, that I know viewers on your side of the pond will be familiar with this story --
paragraphs.cn: 随着杰弗里·爱泼斯坦文件的不断披露，我们了解到更多信息，他的判断力因此受到质疑。我知道你们那边的观众对这个故事很熟悉 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: As one of those that just seem to go away. And I think that is really what's thrown it more recently into a kind of state of crisis. So, yes, it's been a wild ride.
paragraphs.cn: 就像那些似乎永远不会消失的事情一样。我认为这才是最近让局面陷入某种危机状态的原因。所以，是的，这真是一段疯狂的旅程。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I want to -- as he was speaking this morning at Downing Street, I was like, is that music? There was like this music playing behind him. And it turned out it was protesters who were blasting the anthem for the E.U., because it has been almost ten years since the Brexit vote. But since Brexit, there's been the pandemic, the Ukrainian war, I didn't realize this, but an immigration surge as U.K. tried to mitigate some of the concerns of Brexit.
paragraphs.cn: 我想 --  -- 今天早上他在唐宁街讲话时，我心想，那是音乐吗？他身后好像有音乐在播放。结果发现是抗议者在播放欧盟盟歌，因为距离英国脱欧公投已经快十年了。但自脱欧以来，还发生了疫情、乌克兰战争，我没想到的是，英国试图缓解脱欧带来的一些担忧时，出现了移民潮。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, does this put reform in a stronger position? Are we looking at another Labour leader? You know, people have been talking about Andy Burnham because he won -- this is the greater Manchester mayor. Is this still a battle of Labour versus reform? And if so, what does Starmer leaving say about that race?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，这是否让改革党处于更有利的地位？我们是否在关注另一位工党领袖？你知道，人们一直在谈论安迪·伯纳姆，因为他赢得了 --  -- 他是大曼彻斯特市长。这仍然是工党与改革党之间的较量吗？如果是这样，斯塔默的离开对这场竞争意味着什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: I think the first thing to say is, your point about Brexit is right. Essentially, what we've seen over the past decade is British voters trying to, in various ways, push a button marked change, whether that was for Brexit, whether that was for Boris Johnson, who was saying, I'm going to get Brexit done. Then the Tories had a whole host of different scandals. So, then they wanted change again with Keir Starmer. And they feel like they haven't got it.
paragraphs.cn: 我想首先要说的是，你关于脱欧的观点是对的。基本上，过去十年我们看到的是英国选民试图以各种方式按下"改变"按钮，无论是为了脱欧，还是为了鲍里斯·约翰逊 --  -- 他说"我要完成脱欧"。然后保守党出现了一系列不同的丑闻。所以，他们又希望通过基尔·斯塔默实现改变。但他们觉得并没有得到改变。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: So, now, two years in, Andy Burnham is presenting himself as the Labour person who can deliver that longed for change. But I think you're right. I think the real battle that is scaring a lot of Labour MPs is the threat of Nigel Farage and reform. And that's really what led to the most recent crisis was the reform party doing very well in the local elections, a.k.a. the mid-term elections here.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，现在两年过去了，安迪·伯纳姆将自己塑造成能够实现人们渴望的改变的工党人物。但我认为你是对的。我认为真正让许多工党议员感到害怕的威胁是奈杰尔·法拉奇和改革党。而这正是导致最近危机的原因 --  -- 改革党在地方选举（也就是这里的中期选举）中表现非常好。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: And so I think, unless Andy Burnham can -- who looks like he's going to take over for -- as prime minister, the very popular mayor of Manchester here, unless he can really deliver quickly in the next two to three years, there will be people again looking at reform going, well, we've tried everything now. We haven't got the change we wanted. We voted Brexit. That didn't work. We voted for a whole host of different prime ministers. Nothing is working.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，我认为，除非安迪·伯纳姆 --  -- 他看起来将接任首相，他是这里非常受欢迎的曼彻斯特市长 --  -- 除非他能在未来两三年内迅速取得成果，否则人们会再次看向改革党，心想：我们现在什么都试过了。我们没有得到想要的改变。我们投票支持脱欧，但没成功。我们投票选出了一系列不同的首相。什么都不管用。

paragraphs.speaker: Baker
paragraphs.text: And so, reforms pitch is very much, we are the insurgent populist party that can actually smash up the status quo, smash up the establishment and finally deliver change. But it's kind of a -- a bit of a Houdini act, really, because, obviously, Nigel Farage was one of the main architects of Brexit in the first place and many feel that Brexit hasn't been a success.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，改革党的口号非常明确：我们是反叛的民粹主义政党，能够真正打破现状，打破建制，最终实现改变。但这有点像霍迪尼式的逃脱术，因为显然，奈杰尔·法拉奇本身就是脱欧的主要设计者之一，而许多人认为脱欧并不成功。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Well, Patrick Baker, thank you so much. You're going to have a busy day ahead. Appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。好的，帕特里克·贝克，非常感谢。你今天会很忙。感谢你。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now I want to turn to Italy because the president has doubled down on his feud with the prime minister there. And it's basically over this photo. Trump posted on Truth Social that Giorgia Meloni is, quote, "doing poorly in Italy with her level of popularity." So Meloni responded saying, quote, "my popularity is none of your concern, and I suggest you focus on yours." Now, this back and forth basically started when the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 现在我想转向意大利，因为总统加大了他与意大利总理的争执。而这基本上源于这张照片。特朗普在Truth Social上发帖称，乔治娅·梅洛尼"在意大利的人气很低"。梅洛尼回应说，"我的人气不关你的事，我建议你关注自己的人气。"这场来回争执基本上始于美国

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: president accused the Italian prime minister of begging for a photo with him during the G7 Summit last week, which is not how she remembers it. GIORGIA MELONI, ITALIAN PRIME MINISTER (through translator): Donald Trump's statements are completely made up. I am frankly astonished. I don't know why the president of the United States behaves like this toward his allies. There is one thing he should remember, neither I nor Italy ever beg.
paragraphs.cn: 总统上周在七国集团峰会期间指责意大利总理乞求与他合影，但她的记忆并非如此。意大利总理乔治娅·梅洛尼（通过翻译）表示：特朗普的言论完全是捏造的。我对此感到震惊。我不知道为什么美国总统对他的盟友如此行事。他应该记住一件事，我和意大利从未乞求过。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Group chat is back. With friends like these, as we say. I don't know who wants to go first, but why do you think this is persisting the way it is? ELENA SCHNEIDER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "NOTUS": Well, look, Giorgia Meloni is somebody who is seen as a staunch ally of the president. She was one of the only -- I think the only foreign dignitary who attended his inauguration in 2025.
paragraphs.cn: 群聊又回来了。就像我们说的，有这样的朋友。我不知道谁想先发言，但你认为为什么这件事会持续下去？《NOTUS》国家政治记者埃琳娜·施奈德：嗯，乔治娅·梅洛尼被视为总统的坚定盟友。她是唯一一位 --  -- 我想是唯一一位出席他2025年就职典礼的外国政要。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This has been a really warm, strong relationship where she was sort of seen as the leader amongst NATO leaders who could really reach the president. And I think it is really notable that this spat has continued because I think it reflects his larger frustration with NATO allies over this U.S.-Iran conflict. And let's not forget that this spat sort of started a couple of weeks ago when he -- when Donald Trump attacked the pope and then Giorgia Meloni, you know, defended the pope in light of the U.S.-Iran War.
paragraphs.cn: 这段关系一直非常温暖、牢固，她被视为北约领导人中能够真正与总统沟通的领袖。我认为这场争执持续下去非常值得注意，因为这反映了他对北约盟友在美伊冲突问题上的更大不满。别忘了，这场争执大约几周前就开始了，当时唐纳德·特朗普攻击了教皇，然后乔治娅·梅洛尼在美伊战争背景下为教皇辩护。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: There is -- let's go back to the offense. The offense is not backing up Trump when the pope stood up to him. JULIA MANCHESTER, NATIONAL POLITICAL REPORTER, "THE HILL": Absolutely. And we know that Giorgia Meloni has been, like you said. Elena, an ally of the president. She spoke at things like CPAC, for example. She's been a popular figure even within the American conservative movement.
paragraphs.cn: 让我们回到冒犯之处。冒犯之处在于，当教皇对抗特朗普时，她没有支持特朗普。《国会山报》国家政治记者朱莉娅·曼彻斯特：完全正确。我们知道乔治娅·梅洛尼一直像你说的那样，是总统的盟友。她曾在例如CPAC等活动上发言。她甚至在美国保守派运动中也是一个受欢迎的人物。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Because they've backed a lot of right-wing parties in Europe.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。因为他们支持了欧洲许多右翼政党。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's their whole thing.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是他们的全部。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: But, you know, taking a step back from that, it's fascinating to see sort of, you know, I guess, I don't want to say the decline, but maybe the tensions that are rising between Trump and many European leaders, like Meloni, at this time during NATO -- because of the Iraq war, NATO tensions, et cetera.
paragraphs.cn: 但是，退一步看，看到特朗普与许多欧洲领导人（如梅洛尼）之间在此时因伊拉克战争、北约紧张局势等而出现的紧张关系，甚至可以说是某种程度的恶化，这很有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. So, humiliates Meloni.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。所以，羞辱了梅洛尼。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Starmer steps down. He kicks dirt over it by pre-announcing it.
paragraphs.cn: 斯塔默辞职了。他提前宣布这一消息，试图掩盖此事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I don't really know who's left.
paragraphs.cn: 我真的不知道还有谁留下。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: Yes, but at the same time though, in terms of who's left, we see --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但与此同时，就留下的人而言，我们看到 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: Yes. The administration is turning their direction towards Latin America. And yesterday we did see the right wing, Trump-backed conservative leader in Colombia's election win.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。政府正将目光转向拉丁美洲。昨天我们确实看到，哥伦比亚选举中，特朗普支持的右翼保守派领导人获胜。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: So, you're seeing, I think, more of this support from the Trump administration in Latin America as they maybe turn away from Europe.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我认为你会看到特朗普政府在拉丁美洲给予更多支持，因为他们可能正在远离欧洲。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, just in terms of this battle with Italy, the foreign minister actually just canceled a trip to the U.S. where they were going to have a business investment and science forum in Miami. Marco Rubio was supposed to be there. I mean, I don't know how big the business is with Italy, but they're trying to make a point here by saying, we're not showing up to this thing.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，就与意大利的这场争端而言，外交部长实际上刚刚取消了前往美国的行程，他们原计划在迈阿密举办一个商业投资和科学论坛。马可·鲁比奥本应出席。我的意思是，我不知道与意大利的生意有多大，但他们试图通过说"我们不会出席"来表明立场。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And when I looked at the papers this morning there, just a quote here from somebody, this is in, I think, "Libero." This person says, "I hate to say it, but I can't find, and perhaps there isn't, another way to say it: Donald Trump is an idiot. Period." ELEANOR MUELLER, WHITE HOUSE ECONOMY POLICY REPORTER, "SEMAFOR": It's worth reiterating. I mean these are people who agree with Trump ideologically. And we've seen similar splits with folks who, again, think similarly to him in France and Germany.
paragraphs.cn: 今天早上我看报纸时，看到有人引用了一句话，我想是来自《自由报》。这个人说："我不想这么说，但我找不到，也许根本没有别的说法：唐纳德·特朗普是个白痴。句号。"白宫经济政策记者埃莉诺·米勒（来自"Semafor"）表示：值得重申。我的意思是，这些人是在意识形态上同意特朗普的人。我们在法国和德国也看到了类似的分裂，那些人同样与特朗普想法相似。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's not just Carney in Canada who is not on the same page as Trump on many of these things to begin with. It's people who should be his strongest allies abroad. And because --
paragraphs.cn: 不仅仅是加拿大的卡尼在许多事情上与特朗普意见不一。这些人本应是他在国外最坚定的盟友。而且因为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. But she also has wiggle room. Like, when I look at the approval ratings between Trump and Meloni and the pope, like, she's got room -- if you're going to back somebody, are you going to back the guy with 78 percent approval rating or 36?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但她也有回旋余地。比如，当我看到特朗普、梅洛尼和教皇的支持率时，她是有空间的 --  -- 如果你要支持一个人，你会支持支持率78%的人还是36%的人？

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.text: It's a political calculation. They know that Trump is not only polling well in the United States, but he's not polling well in their countries. And so, if you've -- you're looking to pull yourself up a little bit, this is maybe an excuse to do it by leaning into these clashes that Trump seems so willing to start unprompted.
paragraphs.cn: 这是一个政治算计。他们知道特朗普不仅在美国民调表现良好，而且在他们的国家民调并不好。所以，如果你想稍微提升自己，这也许是一个借口，通过卷入特朗普似乎毫无缘由就愿意发起的冲突来实现。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: One more thing. Is it also maybe because, like, they're not -- they didn't fare so well in the tariff conversations. Like, it's not clear to me what the benefit is to be a friend of Trump in this scenario.
paragraphs.cn: 还有一件事。是不是也可能因为，他们在关税谈判中表现不佳？比如，我不清楚在这种情况下成为特朗普的朋友有什么好处。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Like slightly less tariffs or?
paragraphs.cn: 比如稍微少征点关税？

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: I think that's a totally fair point. I mean, look, he has not only obviously gone after just Italy, but a whole host of European countries. Let's not forget where a lot of this also started, which was his attempt to take over Greenland, where he refused to rule out military force in an attempt to go after a NATO ally. So, look, this is -- this has been many years now in this second Trump term where we have seen him really go after allies in addition to who he sees as adversaries.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这完全有道理。我的意思是，你看，他显然不仅针对意大利，还针对许多欧洲国家。别忘了，很多事情也是从那里开始的，比如他试图接管格陵兰岛，当时他拒绝排除使用武力对付北约盟友的可能性。所以，你看，在特朗普的第二个任期内，我们已经看到他在针对他认为的对手的同时，也在针对盟友。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. But oddly self-inflicted. OK, you guys, stay with me. It's now 45 minutes past the hour. This is our morning roundup. Republican Senator Lindsey Graham warning President Trump not to let a critical foreign surveillance tool lapse any longer. Trump stalled his DNI pick, Jay Clayton's confirmation after learning that Democrats were going to let the FISA rule laps, something that Graham argues is playing with fire. SEN.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但奇怪的是自作自受。好了，各位，请继续关注。现在是整点过45分。这是我们的早间综述。共和党参议员林赛·格雷厄姆警告特朗普总统不要让一项关键的外国监控工具再失效。特朗普在得知民主党人将让《外国情报监视法》规则失效后，推迟了他的国家情报总监人选杰伊·克莱顿的确认，格雷厄姆认为这是在玩火。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: LINDSEY GRAHAM (R-SC): And I would urge President Trump to let Clayton testify and that we nominate Clayton and get him affirmed as the new DNI. We don't want FISA to go dark. There's too many threats to our country for 702 to go down. Anybody who owns the shutting down of 702 under FISA will own a future attack against the United States.
paragraphs.cn: 参议员林赛·格雷厄姆（南卡罗来纳州共和党人）：我敦促特朗普总统让克莱顿作证，我们提名克莱顿并让他确认为新的国家情报总监。我们不希望《外国情报监视法》失效。702条款如果失效，对我们的国家威胁太大。任何导致《外国情报监视法》下702条款失效的人，都将为未来针对美国的攻击负责。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Graham also says that the top Democrat on the intelligence committee tells him there are enough Democratic votes to reauthorize FISA if Clayton's nomination is confirmed. And dozens of residents in Louisiana displaced this morning after flood waters from Tropical Storm Arthur. This is reportedly the most flooding the area has seen in decades.
paragraphs.cn: 格雷厄姆还表示，情报委员会的最高民主党人告诉他，如果克莱顿的提名得到确认，将有足够的民主党票数重新授权《外国情报监视法》。今天早上，路易斯安那州数十名居民因热带风暴亚瑟引发的洪水而流离失所。据报道，这是该地区几十年来最严重的洪水。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: We're blessed to not have any fatalities.
paragraphs.cn: 我们很幸运没有人员死亡。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: We just decided to come in today and see what we could do to help.
paragraphs.cn: 我们决定今天过来看看能帮上什么忙。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: What are you going to do?
paragraphs.cn: 你打算做什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: A flood warning for the area is in effect through tonight. And Serena Williams set to make another big return at Wimbledon. Williams accepted a wild card invitation to play singles. The 44-year- old recently returned to competition after announcing her retirement four years back. Williams had already planned to play at Wimbledon, competing in doubles with her sister Venus. And Wimbledon starts next week. Next on CNN THIS MORNING, is Gavin Newsom betting on Biden.
paragraphs.cn: 该地区的洪水警报将持续到今晚。塞雷娜·威廉姆斯准备在温布尔登再次复出。威廉姆斯接受了单打外卡邀请。这位44岁的选手在四年前宣布退役后最近重返赛场。威廉姆斯原本计划参加温布尔登双打比赛，与姐姐维纳斯搭档。温布尔登下周开始。接下来在CNN今晨节目中，加文·纽森押注拜登。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The group chat will be back to discuss the new reporting on why he might be leaning in on the former president's legacy ahead of 2028.
paragraphs.cn: 群聊将再次讨论新的报道，关于他为何可能在2028年前倾向于继承前总统的政治遗产。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, there's a new report in "Axios" providing some insight into a possible Gavin Newsom presidential run. Newsom is reportedly gambling on embracing former President Joe Biden, thinking that will be a popular position among Democrats. Even after Biden had to drop out of his re-election bid, Newsom has still spoken favorably of Biden's presidency. GOV. GAVIN NEWSOM (D-CA): His experiences mirror so many of my fathers. And I see my father in so many ways, the way I see President Biden.
paragraphs.cn: Axios网站发布了一篇新报道，揭示了加文·纽森可能竞选总统的一些内幕。据报道，纽森正押注于支持前总统乔·拜登，认为这将在民主党人中受欢迎。即使在拜登不得不退出连任竞选后，纽森仍对拜登的总统任期赞不绝口。加州州长加文·纽森：他的经历与我父亲的经历非常相似。我在很多方面看到了我的父亲，就像我看到了拜登总统一样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's one of the reasons I was so loyal to the end and I happily will take the arrows on that and defend that. And we'll continue to build on the legacy, I would argue, of our former president, who I think was one of the most successful presidents in the last century, and that is Joe Biden. And I will defend that to my grave.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是我为何忠诚到底的原因之一，我乐意为此承受指责并为之辩护。我们将继续发扬我们前总统的遗产，我认为他是上世纪最成功的总统之一，那就是乔·拜登。我将为此辩护至死。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Group chat is back. So, I don't know how you split the Biden presidency and whatever it's legislative achievements were, or economic achievements were, with the decision to run again and the meltdown that happened in the process.
paragraphs.cn: 群聊又回来了。我不知道如何将拜登的总统任期及其立法成就或经济成就，与他再次参选的决定以及过程中发生的崩溃分开看待。

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.text: Very difficult calculus to do that I think a lot of Democratic hopefuls have decided is not possible. We've seen folks like Brian Schatz (ph) happily critique Biden for his performance across the (INAUDIBLE), not just at the end.
paragraphs.cn: 这是一个非常困难的权衡，我认为许多民主党潜在候选人认为这是不可能的。我们看到像布莱恩·沙茨这样的人，不仅是在最后，而是在整个过程中都乐于批评拜登的表现。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, everyone coming out with their books and, yes, either --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，每个人都在出书，要么 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.text: Exactly. But I don't think anyone can accuse Gavin Newsom of not being a very calculating politician. And so, clearly, he thinks that there is benefit to be found here. Whether that's, you know, Biden's support with black and Latinx voters or what.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。但我认为没有人能指责加文·纽森不是一个精于算计的政治家。所以，显然他认为这里有利可图。无论是拜登在黑人及拉丁裔选民中的支持率，还是其他什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, let's show that. His favorability with black voters right now, 53 percent for Biden remaining and with Latino voters, 40 percent. We know that, especially after black and Latino men and some nominal but helpful numbers went to Trump, that they'd like to bring this community back. And also I want to show you one more set of numbers, favorability ratings for Trump versus Biden, because they're close. And if you're Trump, you don't want them close. But if you're Newsom, you're like, ah, a window.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，让我们展示一下。目前他在黑人选民中的好感度，拜登仍有53%，在拉丁裔选民中为40%。我们知道，尤其是在黑人和拉丁裔男性以及一些名义上但有用的支持转向特朗普之后，他们希望重新赢得这个群体。我还想展示另一组数据，特朗普与拜登的好感度对比，因为两者很接近。如果你是特朗普，你不希望它们接近。但如果你是纽森，你会想，啊，机会来了。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: Not so bad. But, you know, it is certainly, I think, a gamble for Gavin Newsom to do this right now. He is calculating on the point about black and Latino voters. That's certainly a very good point, especially as President Trump loses support from that bloc, from when he gained support with, you know, a certain amount of support in 2024. But I think it comes at a time when you're seeing the Democratic Party start to maybe go in a different direction.
paragraphs.cn: 不算太糟。但你知道，我认为加文·纽森现在这样做确实是一场赌博。他在计算关于黑人和拉丁裔选民这一点。这当然是一个很好的论点，尤其是在特朗普总统失去该群体支持的情况下，他在2024年曾获得一定程度的支持。但我认为，现在正值民主党可能开始转向不同方向的时候。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: You know, you're seeing the popping up of these Democratic socialist candidates in a number of different parts of the country. And, you know, a lot of it could be ideological. But I do also think a lot of it is generational. So, for someone like --
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，你会看到这些民主社会主义候选人在全国多个地方涌现。而且，很多可能是意识形态上的。但我也认为很多是代际问题。所以，对于像 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, he thinks that's the opening for him. Like you can grab all those people who are a little bit older --
paragraphs.cn: 所以，他认为这是他的机会。比如，你可以抓住那些年纪稍大的人 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Aren't so enamored with the DSA or have big questions and say, don't worry, there's still a home for you. People who actually did kind of like Biden's policies.
paragraphs.cn: 那些不那么迷恋民主社会主义者或有很多疑问的人，然后说，别担心，这里仍有你的位置。那些实际上有点喜欢拜登政策的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: And I think that he's very aware, as a lot of those potential 2028 candidates are, that South Carolina is still going to be intimately involved in the early nomination process, a state that has -- that where black voters are an enormously important part of the Democratic primary electorate there.
paragraphs.cn: 而且我认为他很清楚，就像许多潜在的2028年候选人一样，南卡罗来纳州仍将密切参与早期提名过程，这个州的黑人选民是民主党初选选民中极其重要的一部分。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. But an older and slightly more conservative black voters.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但他们是年纪稍大、稍微保守一点的黑人选民。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: Exactly. Exactly what I mean. And that's what rocket launched Joe Biden's own presidency in 2020. And I think that there is a -- an awareness that you can't just alienate those folks by saying, let's throw out Joe Biden altogether. I think he's trying to thread this needle of acknowledging the successes that Joe Biden had. But I also --
paragraphs.cn: 没错。这正是我的意思。而正是这一点在2020年助推了乔·拜登的总统竞选。我认为人们意识到，你不能仅仅通过说"让我们彻底抛弃乔·拜登"来疏远这些人。我认为他试图在承认乔·拜登的成功之间走钢丝。但我也 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And the, reminds me of my dad is very, like, instead of everyone being like, oh, this doddering magoo (ph) is a problem. He's like, you know what, my father. Like this, yes, let's remember.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这让我想起我父亲，他非常像，而不是每个人都像"哦，这个老糊涂是个问题"。他会说，你知道吗，我父亲。就像这样，是的，让我们记住。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: Let's remember the positive feelings about it. And also, to your point about generational, a distinction of, my father, as opposed to, my peer or something like that.
paragraphs.cn: 让我们记住关于它的积极感受。而且，关于代际差异，你提到的是我父亲，而不是我的同龄人或类似的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, here's Hunter Biden. I got to play this one other thing because this Gavin Newsom podcast really great with the bookings. Here's Hunter Biden.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这是亨特·拜登。我得放这段，因为加文·纽森的播客在嘉宾邀请方面真的很棒。这是亨特·拜登。

paragraphs.speaker: Hunter Bidne
paragraphs.text: OK, wait, one thing is just, you've heard dad say it a million times is, I'll come campaign for you or against you, whatever helps most. And, you know, he would say -- GOV. GAVIN NEWSOM (D-CA): And put his finger in my stomach.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，等一下，有一件事，你听爸爸说过无数次了：我会为你或反对你竞选，看哪个最有帮助。而且，你知道，他会说 --  -- 加州州长加文·纽森：然后把手指戳进我的肚子。

paragraphs.speaker: Biden
paragraphs.text: Yes. And he would say it and you'd be like, you know, it was -- it was silly because of course you'd want him to come out. In my case, it's for real.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。他会这么说，然后你会觉得，你知道，这有点傻，因为你当然希望他出来。就我而言，这是真的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, he's joking, but Biden's been on a tour, Junior Biden, trying to rehabilitate. But he has a very clear-eyed way of talking about it.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，他在开玩笑，但拜登一直在巡回，小拜登，试图挽回形象。但他谈论这件事的方式非常清醒。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: He does. And it's interesting to see him on X in particular. And I understand that social media is not always real life --
paragraphs.cn: 确实如此。尤其是在X平台上看到他，很有意思。我知道社交媒体并不总是真实生活 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: But he's becoming a personality and responding to the trolls, to maybe conservatives who are pushing back. So, that's been fascinating to watch.
paragraphs.cn: 但他正在成为一个有个性的人，回应那些喷子，也许是那些反击的保守派。所以，这看起来很有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right, it's Monday. We all have a bunch of reporting we're about to be assigned. And to get ahead of those editor assignments that we don't like, we have ideas of our own. What's in your group chat, Eleanor?
paragraphs.cn: 好了，今天是周一。我们都有很多报道任务要分配。为了避开那些我们不喜欢的编辑任务，我们有自己的想法。埃莉诺，你的群聊里有什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.text: I don't think my editor is going to go over this one.
paragraphs.cn: 我觉得我的编辑不会同意这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Oh, no? No? It's all right.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，不会吧？不会？没关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Mueller
paragraphs.text: We adopted a dog recently. His name is Stanley. And we got him from Old Dominion Humane Society in Fredericksburg, Virginia. They are amazing. He's four years old. He was saved from a hoarder situation. Some lady had 70 dogs in her RV. So, again, shout out to Old Dominion Humane Society. And we are so happy to have Stanley.
paragraphs.cn: 我们最近收养了一只狗。它叫斯坦利。我们从弗吉尼亚州弗雷德里克斯堡的旧自治领人道协会领养了它。他们很棒。它四岁了，是从一个囤积者那里救出来的。有位女士在她的房车里养了70只狗。所以，再次感谢旧自治领人道协会。我们很高兴有斯坦利。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Manchester
paragraphs.text: My group chat is World Cup related.
paragraphs.cn: 我的群聊是关于世界杯的。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: So, Sam Adams' parent company, The Boston Beer Company, said in a news release last year that basically the Tartan Army, which are the Scottish fans that are taking over the city of Boston, drank four times what the company normally stocks during a typical four day weekend, including the Fourth of July. So, once again, the Scots putting Bostonians to shame.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，萨缪尔·亚当斯的母公司波士顿啤酒公司去年在一份新闻稿中说，基本上，占领波士顿的苏格兰球迷"格子军"在包括国庆日在内的典型四天周末里，喝掉了公司通常库存的四倍。所以，苏格兰人再次让波士顿人蒙羞。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This is breaking news in my hometown.
paragraphs.cn: 这是我家乡的突发新闻。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Like, the idea that you could like go to Boston, Fenway.
paragraphs.cn: 比如，你可以去波士顿的芬威球场。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I mean, people know how to drink there, OK. And they just -- they embarrassed them.
paragraphs.cn: 我是说，那里的人很会喝酒，好吧。他们只是 --  -- 他们让那些人难堪了。

paragraphs.speaker: Manchester
paragraphs.text: They're putting them to shame. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 他们让那些人自愧不如。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: They embarrassed them.
paragraphs.cn: 他们让他们难堪了。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: Well, I have to say, mine is also World Cup related. The whole Elmo dust up has been really fun to watch because there's nothing I love more than like a low stakes drama in which like we're all sort of worried about Elmo's safety and security in this moment. But no, seriously, another sort of New York related thing is we've got elections, primaries in New York coming up tomorrow.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我得说，我的话题也和世界杯有关。整个关于艾摩的闹剧看起来很有趣，因为没有什么比这种低风险戏剧更让我喜欢的了，我们都在担心艾摩此刻的安全。但说真的，另一个和纽约相关的事情是，明天纽约将举行选举和初选。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: And I think what's going to come out of those races, those primaries, is going to be so telling about where the Democratic Party is headed.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这些竞选和初选的结果将非常能说明民主党的走向。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: And I think it's going to be a fascinating night that we're all going to be up late watching.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这将是一个迷人的夜晚，我们都会熬夜观看。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And what is Zohran Mamdani's power beyond himself, right?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，佐兰·马姆达尼除了自身之外还有什么影响力呢？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's always the thing. You can have a big win, but do you have the ability to propel others around you? I think that's something people are going to be watching very closely.
paragraphs.cn: 事情总是这样。你可以取得重大胜利，但你有能力推动身边的人吗？我认为这是人们会密切关注的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Schneider
paragraphs.text: And he's sticking his neck out in three different house races. And it's going to be really fascinating to see whether or not that works.
paragraphs.cn: 他在三场不同的众议院竞选中冒险。看看这是否奏效将会非常有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: All right, you guys, thank you so much for being with us. I want to bring people up to speed on one more thing. We are awaiting remarks from the vice president, J.D. Vance. He is in Switzerland for the latest in those U.S. and Iran talks. Basically, in the last 24 hours, he's arrived there to launch the talks. And there's been a lot of friction. Last week we were reporting to you about continued exchange of fire between Hezbollah and Lebanon and Israel.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，各位，非常感谢你们的陪伴。我想再给大家更新一件事。我们正在等待副总统J.D.万斯的讲话。他正在瑞士参加最新一轮美伊谈判。基本上，在过去24小时内，他抵达那里启动了谈判。但双方摩擦不断。上周我们报道了真主党、黎巴嫩和以色列之间持续的交火。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This has become a major sticking point in these talks as Tehran tries to make it part of the overall ceasefire deal. And the Israeli government's frustrations boiling over with the U.S. because they weren't a part of this process at all. So, I think a lot of people are waiting to see how J.D. Vance will thread this needle. And this is day two of these negotiations. The technical teams are supposed to sit down.
paragraphs.cn: 这已成为谈判中的一个主要症结，因为德黑兰试图将其纳入全面停火协议的一部分。而以色列政府对此感到非常沮丧，因为他们根本没有参与这一进程。所以，我认为很多人都在等待J.D.万斯如何化解这一难题。今天是谈判的第二天。技术团队应该会坐下来谈。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: By technical we're talking about the details to be ironed out around enriched uranium and other issues around Iran's nuclear program, which are currently, based on this deal, at status quo. So, please stay with us. We are awaiting J.D. Vance to come to that podium. I'm Audie Cornish. And the headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 所谓技术层面，指的是需要敲定的细节，包括浓缩铀以及伊朗核计划的其他问题，根据目前的协议，这些问题都维持现状。所以，请继续关注我们。我们正在等待J.D.万斯走上讲台。我是奥蒂·科尼什。接下来是新闻提要。
